channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Israel Won't Leave Lebanon As long As Hezbollah "Threatens Us": Netanyahu

media:content.media:description: Netanyahu said that Israel will not leave southern Lebanon until the threat has disappeared.

item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu visited troops in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, vowing that his country's forces would stay in the area as long as Iran-backed Hezbollah remained a "threat".
item.content:encoded: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu visited troops in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, vowing that his country's forces would stay in the area as long as Iran-backed Hezbollah remained a "threat".

item.title: "Civilians Suffering, Children Dying": Ukraine's Drone War Reaches Moscow

media:content.media:description: Russia has not officially confirmed that the satellite centre was hit

item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said the facility plays a role in intelligence operations and helps coordinate Russian military activities in Ukraine.
item.content:encoded: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said the facility plays a role in intelligence operations and helps coordinate Russian military activities in Ukraine.

item.title: 'Can Bring In Legislation': Trump On Supreme Court's Citizenship Ruling

media:content.media:description: The Supreme Court agreed in a majority opinion penned by Chief Justice John Roberts

item.description: In a post on X, US President Donald Trump wrote that the Congress should start working "today" to end the "expensive and unfair" birthright citizenship.
item.content:encoded: In a post on X, US President Donald Trump wrote that the Congress should start working "today" to end the "expensive and unfair" birthright citizenship.

item.title: 300 Excess Deaths Recorded In France During May Heatwave
item.description: French health officials said on Tuesday that there were at least 300 more deaths than expected during a five-day heatwave in May, the country's first of the year.
item.content:encoded: French health officials said on Tuesday that there were at least 300 more deaths than expected during a five-day heatwave in May, the country's first of the year.

item.title: "India In Tough Part Of World, Has Some Rough Neighbours": US Envoy

media:content.media:description: Gor said India operates in a difficult neighbourhood

item.description: Speaking at the US-India Strategic Partnership Forum Leadership Summit in Washington, Gor said India operates in a difficult neighbourhood and hinted at the unpredictability of regional dynamics.
item.content:encoded: Speaking at the US-India Strategic Partnership Forum Leadership Summit in Washington, Gor said India operates in a difficult neighbourhood and hinted at the unpredictability of regional dynamics.

item.title: Trump Asked Officials To Drop Signal. His Inner Circle Ignored His Advice

media:content.media:description: Signal is generally considered one of the safest messaging apps around.

item.description: The State Department released the chat last week, recovered from Rubio and Needham's phones, after a legal challenge by the campaign group Democracy Forward under freedom of information rules.
item.content:encoded: The State Department released the chat last week, recovered from Rubio and Needham's phones, after a legal challenge by the campaign group Democracy Forward under freedom of information rules.

item.title: 14 Children Killed After Coaching Centre Roof Collapses In Pakistan

media:content.media:description: Medical sources said the children killed in the collapse were aged between around four and 12.

item.description: The roof of a tutoring centre collapsed, killing at least 14 children and injuring five in the eastern Pakistani city of Lahore on Tuesday, officials said.
item.content:encoded: The roof of a tutoring centre collapsed, killing at least 14 children and injuring five in the eastern Pakistani city of Lahore on Tuesday, officials said.

item.title: Why Roads In UK Melt At 40 Degrees But Survive India's Gruelling Heat

media:content.media:description: In countries like the UK, road construction has focused on surviving cold conditions

item.description: The difference is not about one country building stronger roads than another. It comes down to the way road materials are selected based on the climate they are expected to handle.
item.content:encoded: The difference is not about one country building stronger roads than another. It comes down to the way road materials are selected based on the climate they are expected to handle.

item.title: 'Worse Than In India, At Least We Have ACs': Tourists Bake In Rome Heatwave

media:content.media:description: Across Italy from north to south, 25 cities were placed on a red heatwave alert on Tuesday

item.description: Around the Colosseum, patches of shade were few and far between. Some tourists waited under a pine tree while others hurried up the Via dei Fori Imperiali, wearing hats or carrying umbrellas to escape the infernal sun.
item.content:encoded: Around the Colosseum, patches of shade were few and far between. Some tourists waited under a pine tree while others hurried up the Via dei Fori Imperiali, wearing hats or carrying umbrellas to escape the infernal sun.

item.title: US Supreme Court Upholds Birthright Citizenship, Rejects Trump's Curbs

media:content.media:description: The court rejected an executive order from Trump that sought to deny birthright citizenship.

item.description: The ruling keeps in place the long-standing judicial reading of the 14th Amendment, which has historically guaranteed citizenship to those born on American soil.
item.content:encoded: The ruling keeps in place the long-standing judicial reading of the 14th Amendment, which has historically guaranteed citizenship to those born on American soil.

item.title: US Supreme Court Upholds Bans On Transgender Athletes In Women Sports

media:content.media:description: The ruling is the latest sign of the court's willingness to side with states on the issue

item.description: US states like Idaho, West Virginia and two dozen other Republican-led states can now enforce measuresrequiring students to compete in public school and college teams according to their sex assigned at birth rather than their gender identity.
item.content:encoded: US states like Idaho, West Virginia and two dozen other Republican-led states can now enforce measuresrequiring students to compete in public school and college teams according to their sex assigned at birth rather than their gender identity.

item.title: Record Number Of 'New Millionaires' In 2025: Report

media:content.media:description: Last year saw nearly one million people worldwide become US dollar millionaires

item.description: Global personal wealth surged in 2025, with a record number of new millionaires, Swiss bank UBS said Tuesday.
item.content:encoded: Global personal wealth surged in 2025, with a record number of new millionaires, Swiss bank UBS said Tuesday.

item.title: "PoK Not Part Of Pakistan": Protesters Warn Islamabad Of Reaching Out To India

media:content.media:description: Protesters claimed the region should not be considered under Pakistan's control

item.description: During the demonstration at Eidgah Ground, speakers claimed that the region should no longer be considered under Pakistan's control and warned about seeking stronger engagement with India.
item.content:encoded: During the demonstration at Eidgah Ground, speakers claimed that the region should no longer be considered under Pakistan's control and warned about seeking stronger engagement with India.

item.title: Roads Turn Into Ice Rivers After Summer Storm In US's Idaho

media:content.media:description: Video shared on social media showed streets covered with ice after torrential thunderstorms and hail.

item.description: The National Weather Service issued a winter storm warning on Saturday for parts of eastern Idaho.
item.content:encoded: The National Weather Service issued a winter storm warning on Saturday for parts of eastern Idaho.

item.title: Broken Promise: Law That Helped Beijing Tighten Its Grip On Hong Kong

media:content.media:description: The Hong Kong Legislative Council was kept out of the process.

item.description: The Hong Kong National Security Law was passed in 2020.
item.content:encoded: The Hong Kong National Security Law was passed in 2020.

item.title: Highlights: Major Police Op In Germany's Stade After 6 Dead In Mass Shooting
item.description: Atleast 6 people were killed after a shooting rocked the northern German city of Stade on Monday, triggering a massive police operation.
item.content:encoded: Atleast 6 people were killed after a shooting rocked the northern German city of Stade on Monday, triggering a massive police operation.

item.title: Videos: Roads Melt, Tram Tracks Buckle As Europe Struggles With Heatwave

media:content.media:description: The scorching heat has already set records in Poland, the Czech Republic and Germany

item.description: According to the World Health Organization (WHO), over 1,300 excess deaths had been recorded since 21 June "linked to high temperatures in Europe".
item.content:encoded: According to the World Health Organization (WHO), over 1,300 excess deaths had been recorded since 21 June "linked to high temperatures in Europe".

item.title: No Direct US-Iran Talks To Take Place In Doha, Says Qatar
item.description: Mediator Qatar said on Tuesday that no high-level meetings or direct talks between the United States and Iran were planned in Doha after Washington said it had sent a top delegation for negotiations.
item.content:encoded: Mediator Qatar said on Tuesday that no high-level meetings or direct talks between the United States and Iran were planned in Doha after Washington said it had sent a top delegation for negotiations.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | US' Scott Bessent Has A New 'Doctrine', And It Leaves India With Hard Choices
item.description: Scott Bessent in a recent WSJ opinion piece has explained the so-called American economic 'statecraft'. The doctrine should raise concerns in India.
item.content:encoded: Scott Bessent in a recent WSJ opinion piece has explained the so-called American economic 'statecraft'. The doctrine should raise concerns in India.

item.title: Rare Dinosaur Fossil Found In Museum Drawer Decades After 1985 Discovery

media:content.media:description: Dinosaur fossils are rare to find in Antarctica because of the unforgiving ice caps.

item.description: A rare titanosaur tail bone, tucked in a drawer since 1985, has revealed dinosaurs once roamed Antarctica when it was lush and forested.
item.content:encoded: A rare titanosaur tail bone, tucked in a drawer since 1985, has revealed dinosaurs once roamed Antarctica when it was lush and forested.
